caption: Job
description: The Job object provides information about a scheduled job or task, including cron jobs, Windows Task Scheduler tasks, and cloud-managed scheduled tasks. It encompasses attributes that describe the properties, schedule, and execution status of the job.
name: job

cmd_line.description: The command line executed by the job.

created_time.description: The time when the job was created.

file.description: The file or executable that the job runs.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the job is enabled and eligible to run.

last_run_time.description: The time when the job was last run.

modified_time.description: The time when the job was last modified.

name.description: The name of the job. For example, the Windows Task Scheduler task name or cron job label.

next_run_time.description: The time when the job will next be run.

run_count.description: The number of times the job has been executed.

run_state.description: The run state of the job, normalized to the caption of the run_state_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the source.

run_state_id.description: The normalized run state identifier of the job.

1.caption: Ready
1.description: The job is ready to run at its next scheduled time or on trigger.

2.caption: Queued
2.description: The job is queued for execution.

4.caption: Stopped
4.description: The job was stopped before completion.

6.caption: Failed
6.description: The job finished execution with an error.

7.caption: Disabled
7.description: The job is disabled and will not run.

trigger.description: The trigger or schedule that initiates the job. For example, a cron expression, Windows Task Scheduler trigger XML, or cloud schedule rule.

user.description: The user context under which the job runs, or the user that created the job.
